title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
description: A beautiful story of a legendary wizard told in his own words. Kvothe recounts his extraordinary life from his childhood to his days at the University.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: Set on the desert planet Arrakis, Dune is the story of the boy Paul Atreides, who would become the mysterious man known as Muad'Dib.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
description: A lone astronaut must save the earth from disaster in this propulsive science thriller full of solvable problems and unexpected discoveries.

title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: Between life and death there is a library, and within that library, the shelves go on forever. Every book provides a chance to try another life you could have lived.

title: Fantastic Mr. Fox
author: Roald Dahl
description: The clever Mr. Fox outwits three mean, dim-witted farmers who are determined to capture him to protect their livestock.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: Bilbo Baggins, a respectable hobbit, is swept into an epic quest to reclaim the dwarven city of Erebor from the dragon Smaug.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
description: A memoir about a young woman who grew up in a survivalist family in the mountains of Idaho and went on to earn a PhD from Cambridge University.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical novel about a young Andalusian shepherd who dreams of traveling the world in search of a treasure as worldly as any ever found.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: A revolutionary system to get 1% better every day. James Clear distills the most fundamental information about habit formation, offering a science-based self-improvement system.

title: Klara and the Sun
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: Told from the perspective of Klara, an Artificial Friend who observes human behavior with precision and a certain innocence.

title: The Song of Achilles
author: Madeline Miller
description: A tale of gods, kings, immortal fame, and the human heart — a retelling of the Trojan War through the eyes of Patroclus.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: Kahneman reveals the two systems that drive the way we think: System 1 is fast, intuitive, and emotional; System 2 is slower, more deliberative, and more logical.

title: Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow
author: Gabrielle Zevin
description: A story about the nature of identity, disability, failure, and success — about how we know and don't know those we love.

title: Chained to the Alien
author: J.K. Pendragon
description: A story about a young man who finds his quiet life turned upside down when he discovers an alien captive and chooses to help him.

title: Vital Voices
author: Alyse Nelson
description: Profiles of courageous women leaders from around the globe who are transforming their communities and advocating for human rights.

title: The Three-Body Problem
author: Liu Cixin
description: Set against the backdrop of China's Cultural Revolution, a secret military project sends signals into space to establish contact with aliens.

title: The Winter's Tale
author: William Shakespeare
description: A complex tragicomedy involving jealousy, a lost princess, a statue that comes to life, and eventual reconciliation.

title: Goethe's Morning Land (West-Eastern Diwan)
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
description: A collection of poems inspired by Persian literature, exploring themes of love, spirituality, and the bridging of Eastern and Western cultures.

title: Max's Dragon Shirt
author: Rosemary Wells
description: Max wants a fierce dragon shirt, but his mother insists on buying him new pants instead, leading to a classic shopping mall tantrum.

title: Northanger Abbey and Persuasion
author: Jane Austen
description: Nine beautiful, mind-bending stories about technology, time, and what it means to be human in an uncanny universe.
